name: Potato Frittata for Brunch (Pg&e "Spotlight")
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Pimento, or Bell Peppers, Red Roasted - chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel potatoes and slice very thinly. Set aside.
2. Slice onions very thinly.
3. Heat oil in a 10-inch nonstick, oven-proof skillet.
4. Layer potato slices in the pan and cook over medium heat for 5 minutes. Lower heat if potatoes begin to brown.
5. Using a pancake turner, turn potato slices over and sprinkle onions on top. Cook for an additional 5 minutes.
6. Repeat step 5, turning onion with potato, until potatoes have cooked for a total of 20 minutes. Onions will be translucent, and potatoes will be soft and slightly golden.
7. Beat eggs with a whisk in a large bowl. Add pimento or chopped red bell peppers, salt, and pepper; beat again.
8. Pour the egg mixture into the skillet over the potato and onion layers.
9. As the eggs begin to cook and become firm around the edges, carefully lift the edge with a flexible spatula, allowing uncooked egg to slide under the cooked egg.
10. Cook for an additional 5 minutes on top of the stove.
11. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese, place the skillet in the oven, and bake at 350°F (175°C) for 15 minutes until golden.
12. Loosen the frittata from the skillet edge with a spatula and slide onto a large serving plate. Cut into wedges and serve. The frittata can be enjoyed either warm or at room temperature.
